company.dd:
We are Uber. The go-getters. The kind of people who are relentless about our mission to help people go anywhere and get anything and earn their way. Movement is what we power. It’s our lifeblood. It runs through our veins. It’s what gets us out of bed each morning. It pushes us to constantly reimagine how we can move better. For you. For all the places you want to go. For all the things you want to get. For all the ways you want to earn. Across the entire world. In real time. At the incredible speed of now.

The idea for Uber was born on a snowy night in Paris in 2008, and ever since then our DNA of reimagination and reinvention carries on. We’ve grown into a global platform powering flexible earnings and the movement of people and things in ever expanding ways. We’ve gone from connecting rides on 4 wheels to 2 wheels to 18-wheel freight deliveries. From takeout meals to daily essentials to prescription drugs to just about anything you need at any time and earning your way. From drivers with background checks to real-time verification, safety is a top priority every single day. At Uber, the pursuit of reimagination is never finished, never stops, and is always just beginning.

rounds.name: Online Assessment
rounds.roundDescription: This was an online coding assessment where I had to solve at least 3.5 out of 4 problems to pass. One of the problems involved implementing a Quad Tree.

question.title: Implement a Quad Tree
question.statement:
Design and implement a Quad Tree data structure. A Quad Tree is a tree data structure in which each internal node has exactly four children. Quad trees are most often used to partition a two-dimensional space by recursively subdividing it into four quadrants or regions.

Your implementation should include:

*   A constructor that initializes the tree with a given bounding box (e.g., coordinates of the top-left and bottom-right corners).
*   An `insert` method to add a point or object into the tree.
*   A `query` method to find all points or objects within a given search area.
*   Consider how to handle overlapping objects or objects that span multiple quadrants.

###### Input Format:

Input will typically involve a series of points (x, y coordinates) or objects with bounding boxes to be inserted, and then search queries defined by rectangular regions.

###### Output Format:

The `query` method should return a list of all points or objects found within the specified search area.

###### Constraints:

*   The coordinate system and bounds will be clearly defined.
*   Assume coordinates are integers.
*   The number of points to be inserted and queries will be up to 10^5.
*   The tree should efficiently handle insertions and queries without excessive depth.

rounds.name: DSA Screening
rounds.roundDescription: This coding round focused on a hard-difficulty coding problem, requiring me to implement a working solution and explain my approach, including time and space complexity.

rounds.name: DSA Round
rounds.roundDescription: This round involved solving another hard-difficulty coding problem, with a strong emphasis on writing working code, handling all test cases, and thoroughly explaining the approach with its time and space complexities.

rounds.name: Low-Level Design Round
rounds.roundDescription: I was given a machine coding problem to design and implement a system with specific features. The evaluation focused heavily on producing runnable code, utilizing appropriate design patterns, ensuring concurrency, and applying the most optimal algorithms for the core machine logic.

question.title: Design and Implement a Multi‑Threaded Topic‑Based Message Broker / Kafka/ Pub-Sub / Queue
question.statement:
Design a thread‑safe, topic‑based message broker that supports publish‑subscribe semantics. The broker should allow multiple publishers and subscribers to operate concurrently and enable subscribers to replay messages by resetting their offset.

- Maintain a separate queue for each topic.
- Publishers can publish a message to a specific topic.
- Subscribers can subscribe to one or more topics and should receive every message published to those topics after they subscribe.
- When a message is published to a topic, all active subscribers of that topic must receive the message.
- Each subscriber tracks its own offset per topic; the broker must provide an operation to reset a subscriber’s offset to any previous position so the subscriber can re‑read messages.
- The system must handle concurrent publish and subscribe operations safely, ensuring no race conditions or data corruption.
- Subscribers should be able to consume messages in parallel without blocking each other.
- Assume messages are simple strings; persistence, replication, and fault tolerance are out of scope.
question.dl: 632. Design and Implement a Multi‑Threaded Topic‑Based Message Broker / Kafka/ Pub-Sub / Queue

rounds.name: Leadership Round
rounds.roundDescription: This round consisted of situational behavioral questions, where I had to describe how I would act or had acted in various workplace scenarios. The goal was to demonstrate my capabilities as an L4 level engineer through my responses.

rounds.name: High-Level Design Round
rounds.roundDescription: This round involved designing an order processing system, with a specific focus on displaying top-k items based on their popularity score and their detailed information. The discussion covered requirements gathering, high-level architecture, API design, and evaluating different database choices.

question.title: Design E-commerce Backend for Product Browsing and Popularity Ranking
question.statement:
Design a backend system for an e-commerce website that focuses exclusively on the merchandise browsing experience. The system should allow users to view products and must actively use real-time popularity data to select and highlight top-trending items. The purchase process, including cart management and checkout, is explicitly out of scope for this design.

Your design should address the following considerations:

*   **Event Processing**: How will the system process a vast amount of user interaction events (e.g., 'Product added to Cart', 'Product viewed', 'Product wishlisted', 'Product purchased') to derive popularity metrics?
*   **Data Storage**: Where should the product catalog data be stored? Provide an example schema or data structure for a product entry.
*   **Popularity Ranking**: How will the 'top items' be identified and updated in near real-time based on the processed events?
*   **System Priorities**: Discuss whether consistency or availability should be prioritized for different components of this system, and justify your choices.
*   **Scalability**: How would you ensure the system can handle a high volume of concurrent users browsing products and a large stream of events?
*   **Component Choices**: Justify your choices for technologies like message queues (e.g., Kafka), databases (e.g., MongoDB), and caching layers (e.g., Redis).

###### Requirements:

1.  **Core Functionality**: Display product listings, allow browsing by categories/filters, and feature a section for 'Top Items'.
2.  **Event Ingestion**: Design an efficient mechanism to ingest high-volume, real-time user interaction events.
3.  **Popularity Calculation**: Implement a method to aggregate and calculate product popularity based on these events.
4.  **Real-time Updates**: Ensure that the 'Top Items' list reflects recent trends with minimal latency.
5.  **Data Modeling**: Propose a simplified data model for product information, explicitly stating what would be stored.
6.  **Architectural Choices**: Explain the roles of various components (e.g., API Gateway, Load Balancers, Databases, Caches, Message Brokers) in your design.
question.dl: 3012. Design E-commerce Backend for Product Browsing and Popularity Ranking

dl: 1254. Uber Interview Experience for Software Development Engineer 2
summary:
In total, there were 6 rounds of interviews evaluating various skills as mentioned in each round below:

👉 Round 1 - Online Assessment (90 minutes): This was an online coding assessment where I had to solve at least 3.5 out of 4 problems to pass. One of the problems involved implementing a Quad Tree.

👉 Round 2 - DSA Screening (60 minutes): This coding round focused on a hard-difficulty coding problem, requiring me to implement a working solution and explain my approach, including time and space complexity.

👉 Round 3 - DSA Round (60 minutes): This round involved solving another hard-difficulty coding problem, with a strong emphasis on writing working code, handling all test cases, and thoroughly explaining the approach with its time and space complexities.

👉 Round 4 - Low-Level Design Round (60 minutes): I was given a machine coding problem to design and implement a system with specific features. The evaluation focused heavily on producing runnable code, utilizing appropriate design patterns, ensuring concurrency, and applying the most optimal algorithms for the core machine logic.

👉 Round 5 - Leadership Round (60 minutes): This round consisted of situational behavioral questions, where I had to describe how I would act or had acted in various workplace scenarios. The goal was to demonstrate my capabilities as an L4 level engineer through my responses.

👉 Round 6 - High-Level Design Round (60 minutes): This round involved designing an order processing system, with a specific focus on displaying top-k items based on their popularity score and their detailed information. The discussion covered requirements gathering, high-level architecture, API design, and evaluating different database choices.
